actual_current_surface_status: open / Ward physical-readout repair not closed
next_positive_target: write a new operator-matching theorem that derives the source/HS normalization, chirality projection, scalar LSZ factor, and absence of extra factors in one runner-checked chain without y_t_bare := <0|H_unit|tt> as a definition
verdict: The repo contains prose and arithmetic for the Ward physical-readout repair, but the current audit surface does not close it.  The old Ward theorem remains audited_renaming; the SSB/source matching note is audited_renaming; chirality/H_unit support is failed or conditional; and the scalar LSZ/color-projection bridge remains audited_conditional.

ward_status.load_bearing_step: On the canonical surface (g_bare = 1), the framework's bare Yukawa coupling y_t_bare is DEFINED as the unit-norm-state matrix element of the H_unit operator between the vacuum and a single top-pair state.
ward_status.verdict_rationale: Issue: The load-bearing move defines y_t_bare as the H_unit matrix element and then identifies that matrix element with the top Yukawa readout. Why this blocks: The source note and runner verify the scalar-singlet algebra, Clebsch-Gordan normalization, Fierz coefficients, and tadpole cancellation, but they do not derive the physical observable bridge from independent retained inputs. Repair target: Supply a retained theorem and runner check constructing the map from the composite H_unit matrix element to the SM top Yukawa vertex/readout, including the common tadpole dressing. Claim boundary until fixed: The note supports a tree-level structural identification/algebraic normalization on the canonical surface, not a first-principles derivation of the physical top Yukawa coupling.
